title: Child mortality rate by cause
subtitle: Mortality rates for children under five per 100,000 individuals.
caption:
Child mortality rate by cause

Mortality rates for children under five per 100,000 individuals.

This horizontal bar chart displays global child mortality rates by cause for the year 2023, measured as deaths per 100,000 children under the age of five. The causes are ranked in descending order of mortality rate. Neonatal disorders represent the leading cause, with a rate of 253.2, which is substantially higher than any other category. Pneumonia follows as the second highest cause at 94.8, and neonatal asphyxia and trauma is third at 87.4. Congenital birth defects and malaria occupy the next positions with rates of 71.3 and 63.5 respectively. Diarrheal diseases account for 49.3 deaths per 100,000, while other neonatal disorders contribute 29.4. A cluster of causes including measles, nutritional deficiencies, and meningitis show rates between 13.5 and 18.4. The lower portion of the chart features a dense grouping of causes with values below 10, including tuberculosis, drowning, and COVID-19, which all sit at or near 7.4. The list continues downward through neoplasms, road injuries, and cardiovascular diseases, ending with diabetes mellitus at the bottom with a rate of 0.4. The visual arrangement highlights a steep drop-off after the top cause, with the remaining bars decreasing in length progressively down the list.
